title: nmap
html:
nmap

Port scanning and service enumeration. First tool on every box to map the attack surface.

Quick Scans

# Fast full-port initial scan
nmap -sC -sV -p- --min-rate 5000 -oA nmap/full <target>

# Top 1000 ports (fastest first look)
nmap -sC -sV -oA nmap/quick <target>

# Common HTB ports
nmap -sC -sV -p 21,22,25,53,80,88,110,135,139,143,389,443,445,464,593,636,1433,3268,3269,3389,5985,5986,8080,8443,9389 -oA nmap/targeted <target>

Scan Types

# TCP SYN scan (default, fast, stealthy)
nmap -sS <target>

# TCP Connect scan (no root needed)
nmap -sT <target>

# UDP scan (slow — limit ports)
nmap -sU --top-ports 100 <target>

# Combined TCP + UDP
nmap -sS -sU --top-ports 200 <target>

# ACK scan (firewall rule mapping)
nmap -sA <target>

Version & Script Detection

# Version detection
nmap -sV --version-intensity 5 <target>

# Default scripts
nmap -sC <target>

# Specific script
nmap --script=smb-enum-shares -p 445 <target>

# Multiple scripts
nmap --script=http-enum,http-headers,http-methods -p 80 <target>

# Script with args
nmap --script=smb-enum-shares --script-args smbusername=user,smbpassword=pass -p 445 <target>

NSE Script Categories

# Safe scripts (won't crash target)
nmap --script=safe <target>

# Vulnerability detection
nmap --script=vuln <target>

# Discovery (enum everything)
nmap --script=discovery <target>

# Combine categories
nmap --script="vuln and safe" <target>

# Useful individual scripts
nmap --script=smb-enum-shares,smb-enum-users,smb-os-discovery -p 445 <target>
nmap --script=http-enum,http-title,http-robots.txt -p 80 <target>
nmap --script=dns-brute --script-args dns-brute.domain=target.htb <target>
nmap --script=ldap-rootdse -p 389 <target>
nmap --script=ms-sql-info,ms-sql-config -p 1433 <target>

Output Formats

# All formats at once (recommended)
nmap -oA nmap/scan <target>

# Normal output
nmap -oN nmap/scan.nmap <target>

# Grepable output
nmap -oG nmap/scan.gnmap <target>

# XML output (for searchsploit --nmap)
nmap -oX nmap/scan.xml <target>

Performance Tuning

# Timing templates (T0=paranoid ... T5=insane)
nmap -T4 <target>

# Min packet rate
nmap --min-rate 5000 <target>

# Max retries (reduce for speed)
nmap --max-retries 2 <target>

# Host timeout
nmap --host-timeout 30m <target>

# Aggressive combo
nmap -T4 --min-rate 5000 --max-retries 2 <target>

Firewall Evasion

# Fragment packets
nmap -f <target>

# ACK scan (map firewall rules)
nmap -sA <target>

# Source port (DNS/HTTP often allowed)
nmap --source-port 53 <target>
nmap --source-port 80 <target>

# Decoy scan
nmap -D RND:10 <target>

# Idle/zombie scan
nmap -sI zombie_ip <target>

# MTU manipulation
nmap --mtu 24 <target>

Port Specification

# Specific ports
nmap -p 22,80,443 <target>

# Port range
nmap -p 1-1024 <target>

# All ports
nmap -p- <target>

# Top N ports
nmap --top-ports 200 <target>

# Exclude ports
nmap --exclude-ports 25,135 <target>

Grep-Fu on Results

# Extract open ports from grepable output
grep -oP '\d+/open' nmap/scan.gnmap | cut -d/ -f1 | sort -un | tr '\n' ','

# Feed into searchsploit
searchsploit --nmap nmap/scan.xml
